title: Command Lifecycle, Main Boundary, and Decomposition · Behavior Case: Dataflow Ownership
summary: A one-file script reads global arguments during import, mixes file access with counting and printing, cannot be tested without exiting, and changes behavior when imported. Predict the user decision, input identity, text policy, expected report, failure boundary, and evidence that could disprove the proposed implementation before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the dataflow ownership increment in testable command lifecycle and function map. Learners progress throug…
objectives: Trace each value from argument through bytes, text, tokens, statistics, report records, and output without hidden mutation.
Keep deterministic text transformations pure and pass filesystem, stream, clock, and terminal effects through a thin shell.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: bookbot-dataflow-ownership — Trace each value from argument through bytes, text, tokens, statistics, report records, and output without hidden mutation.
# Evidence: bookbot-worked-bookbot-cli-lifecycle-contract-5
# Competency: bookbot-worked-bookbot-cli-lifecycle-contract-5.
# Case 9036: a digital-humanities corpus revision.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed boundary, causal evidence, repair, regression test, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: install the built wheel in a fresh environment and rerun success, invalid-argument, and decode-failure cases.
def worked_bookbot_cli_lifecycle_contract_5(import_safe: bool = True, pure_stages: int = 7, side_effect_boundaries: int = 2, hidden_globals: int = 0, exit_status: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "corpusworked5",
        "policy5dekrow",
        "revision9036",
        "commandworked59036",
        "report5dekrow9036",
        "test9036worked5",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if not import_safe or pure_stages < 1:
        return False, pure_stages, "lifecycle"
    if side_effect_boundaries < 1 or side_effect_boundaries > 3:
        return False, side_effect_boundaries, "effect-shell"
    if hidden_globals > 0:
        return False, hidden_globals, "hidden-state"
    if exit_status != 0:
        return False, exit_status, "command-failure"
    assert pure_stages > side_effect_boundaries
    return True, pure_stages, "testable-command"

steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Dataflow Ownership: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory Bookbot team handles case 3935 during an offline public-library reading report. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate import-safe definitions, a pure analysis core, explicit effects, and command status; test empty, malformed, Unicode, tied, large, changed-policy, and rejected cases before accepting output; then split one word and one UTF-8 sequence across stream chunks and compare batch output byte-for-byte. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Trace each value from argument through bytes, text, tokens, statistics, report records, and output without hidden mutation.

Evidence target: Bookbot and Python Defend the Dataflow Ownership model · Theory Dataflow Ownership.
steps.why: Retrieving Dataflow Ownership through the concrete testable command lifecycle and function map case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Bookbot and Python Defend the Dataflow Ownership model · Theory Dataflow Ownership, keep Dataflow Ownership connected to executable Python text-analysis, command, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Trace each value from argument through bytes, text, tokens, statistics, report records, and output without hidden mutation.

hints: For Bookbot and Python Defend the Dataflow Ownership model · Theory Dataflow Ownership, name the stakeholder, corpus authority, revision, path, bytes, encoding, Unicode, token, count, rank, report, command, test, package, release, and transfer boundary, Dataflow Ownership precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Bookbot and Python Defend the Dataflow Ownership model · Theory Dataflow Ownership, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, formatting, stdout, stderr, status, test, package, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory Bookbot team handles case 3935 during an offline public-library reading report. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate import-safe definitions, a pure analysis core, explicit effects, and command status; test empty, malformed, Unicode, tied, large, changed-policy, and rejected cases before accepting output; then split one word and one UTF-8 sequence across stream chunks and compare batch output byte-for-byte. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Trace each value from argument through bytes, text, tokens, statistics, report records, and output without hidden mutation.

For Bookbot and Python Defend the Dataflow Ownership model · Theory Dataflow Ownership, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Python names own independent copies of every mutable value.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: bookbot-dataflow-ownership-1u5grz.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: bookbot-dataflow-ownership-1u5grz(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_bookbot_dataflow_ownership_1u5grz\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not import_safe)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?side_effect_boundaries > 3)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?hidden_globals > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?exit_status != 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
